title: That Women Are But Men's Shadows by Ben Jonson — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
Follow a shadow, it still flies you;
Seem to fly it, it will pursue:
So court a mistress, she denies you;
Let her alone, she will court you.
Say, are not women truly then
Styled but the shadows of us men?

At morn and even shades are longest,
At noon they are or short or none;
So men at weakest, they are strongest,
But grant us perfect, they're not known.
Say, are not women truly then
Styled but the shadows of us men?
meaning: "That Women Are But Men's Shadows" by Ben Jonson is a deep poem consisting of 13 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Follow a shadow, it still flies you; Seem to fly it, it will pursue:...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Ben Jonson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
